label: PI-005 satisfier — process v2 schema growth end-to-end (migration + access + REST + UI + tests). Final of the three PI-003/004/005 design-phase planning items; PI-003 (persona) shipped under SES-082, PI-004 (the four-sibling cohort) shipped under SES-086, and this session RESOLVES PI-005 via slice A's atomic edge+flip behavior — the `resolves_planning_items` section below flips PI-005 from Open to Resolved at apply time. NOT a new entity type — this build grows the existing `process` methodology entity additively per `methodology-schema-specs/process-v2.md` v2.0. Migration 0018 adds six new TEXT NULL columns to the existing `processes` table (`process_steps`, `process_triggers`, `process_outcomes`, `process_edge_cases`, `process_frequency`, `process_duration_estimate` per spec §3.2.2) — Phase 3 detailed-process content, plain text in v0.6+ with structured JSON shapes deferred to v0.7+. Three new outbound vocabulary kinds registered in `vocab.py` and admitted in the `refs.relationship_kind` CHECK: `process_performed_by_persona` (process → persona; persona is live as of PI-003), `process_touches_field` (process → field; field is live as of PI-004 first slice), `process_touches_entity` (process → entity; promoted from v0.4-anticipated to live registration). All three `_kinds_for_pair` clauses ship active because all three target types are live in `ENTITY_TYPES`. No data backfill — existing v0.4 process records acquire NULL for all six new columns and continue to validate as legal v2 records (acceptance criterion 3). No status change — `process_classification` enum and the four-value lifecycle preserved verbatim. Master pane unchanged per spec §3.6.2 (column growth deferred to v0.7+). Detail pane grows by inserting a 'Phase 3 — Detailed Process Definition' group with six CollapsibleSection widgets between the v0.4 classification-rationale row and the Internal notes section per spec §3.6.3; each section's body collapses by default when its column is NULL/empty. Edit dialog includes editors for the six new fields via the new `include_phase3` keyword on `process_fields()`; Create dialog unchanged per spec §3.6.4 (Phase 3 content is post-create work). Migration 0018 fully reversible per spec §4.5 — downgrade drops the six columns and reverts the CHECK with a guarded DELETE of any rows holding the new kinds (lossy recovery posture documented). Test suite: 1854 baseline → 1876 (+22 new, 0 regressions), 3 skipped pre-existing.

session.topics_covered: Execution of the build prompt's 17 numbered steps for the process v2 schema growth per process-v2.md v2.0: (1) Pre-flight checks: working directory, git identity, file existence, persona/field/entity table presence (all live), test baseline (1854), Alembic head (0017_v0_5_create_test_specs_table), API health, PI-005 status (Open), latest IDs (SES-087/CONV-057/DEC-287); (2) Alembic migration 0018_v0_8_process_v2_growth.py modeled on the 0011/0012/0017 CHECK-extension pattern — adds six new TEXT NULL columns to `processes` via `batch_alter_table` and extends `refs.relationship_kind` CHECK to admit `process_performed_by_persona`, `process_touches_field`, `process_touches_entity` (alphabetically sorted in the CHECK constant); fully reversible downgrade() drops the columns and reverts the CHECK with a guarded DELETE of any rows holding the new kinds; (3) `Process` SQLAlchemy model in `access/models.py` grows by six `Mapped[str | None]` columns (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046); class docstring updated with the v0.8 schema growth note; no new `__table_args__` entries (all six columns are plain TEXT nullable with no CHECK constraints); (4) `vocab.py` additions — three new kinds added to `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS` with a comment block citing PI-005 and process-v2.md §3.3.2; three new clauses added to `_kinds_for_pair` for `(process, persona)`, `(process, field)`, `(process, entity)` — all three ship active because all three target types are live in `ENTITY_TYPES`; docstring updated with three new bullet entries; (5) `access/repositories/process.py` — `_PATCHABLE_FIELDS` extended with six new keys (`steps`, `triggers`, `outcomes`, `edge_cases`, `frequency`, `duration_estimate`); `_new_process_row`, `_insert_with_autoassign`, `create_process` all extended with six new keyword-only optional parameters defaulting to `None`; `update_process` (PUT) extended with the same six parameters and applies them wholesale per PUT semantics (omitted-from-body clears to NULL); `patch_process` (PATCH) extended with six new `if 'key' in fields:` branches per spec §3.5.2 PATCH-null/empty-string distinction; docstrings updated; (6) `api/schemas.py` — `ProcessCreateIn`, `ProcessReplaceIn`, `ProcessPatchIn` all gain six optional `str | None = None` fields with docstrings noting create-time optionality (spec §3.6.4), PUT-clear semantics under full replace (§3.5.2), and PATCH-null vs PATCH-empty vs PATCH-omit distinctions (§3.5.2); (7) `api/routers/processes.py` create() and replace() pass the six new fields through to the repository; PATCH handler unchanged (the dispatcher already strips the `process_` prefix); no new endpoints; (8) UI client (`ui/client.py`) needs no changes — its methods take dict bodies and pass through to the JSON body verbatim; (9) No sidebar / main_window edits per spec §3.6.1; (10) `ui/panels/processes.py` detail pane gains a 'Phase 3 — Detailed Process Definition' group label and six `CollapsibleSection` widgets between the v0.4 classification-rationale row and the Internal notes section per spec §3.6.3; section body widgets named `process_<field>_value`, parent sections named `process_<field>_section`; each section defaults expanded if the underlying column has non-whitespace content, collapsed otherwise; module-level `_PHASE3_SECTION_SPECS` list carries the (field_key, label, placeholder) triples; the inline-rendering approach was taken rather than the optional bundled `ProcessExecutionContextSection` composite widget (spec §3.6.3 / §3.8.1 leaves the choice to the build conversation); (11) `ui/dialogs/_process_schema.py` — new module-level `_PHASE3_FIELDS_SPECS` list, new `_phase3_fields()` helper returning six `FieldSchema` entries with `widget='text'` (multi-line) and placeholders matching the spec §3.6.3 text, new `include_phase3: bool = False` keyword on `process_fields()` appending the Phase 3 fields when True; (12) `ui/dialogs/process_crud.py` — `ProcessEditDialog` calls `process_fields(client, include_identifier=True, include_phase3=True)`; `ProcessCreateDialog` unchanged (the `include_phase3` defaults to False per spec §3.6.4); PATCH process_steps='1. Receive trigger. 2. Validate inputs. 3. Execute. 4. Notify outcome.' updates process_steps and leaves process_triggers null; PATCH revert to null clears it; direct DB inspection of refs CHECK confirms all three new kinds admitted; (16) close-out authoring with the SES-088 / CONV-058 / DEC-288 identifier block.
session.summary: PI-005 satisfier (process v2 schema growth) shipped end-to-end. RESOLVES PI-005 via the resolves_planning_items section's atomic edge+flip behavior — this is the final of the three PI-003/004/005 design-phase trio. NOT a new entity type — additive growth of the existing `process` methodology entity per `methodology-schema-specs/process-v2.md` v2.0. Three new outbound vocabulary kinds — `process_performed_by_persona` (process → persona), `process_touches_field` (process → field), `process_touches_entity` (process → entity, promoted from v0.4-anticipated to live registration) — DEC-289. All three target types are live in `ENTITY_TYPES` so every `_kinds_for_pair` clause activates immediately. The agent followed the build prompt's 17 numbered steps in order; the build is purely additive (six new TEXT NULL columns on the existing processes table, three new vocab kinds in `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS`, three new `_kinds_for_pair` clauses, six new optional kwargs on the access-layer functions, six new optional fields on the three Pydantic schemas, two new Pydantic-router pass-through arguments, one new keyword on `process_fields()`, one new module-level spec list and a six-iteration `CollapsibleSection` loop in the detail pane). commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-005 — process v2 schema growth (six TEXT columns + three vocab kinds + UI)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-005 — process v2 schema growth (six TEXT columns + three vocab kinds + UI)

Grows the existing process methodology entity per
methodology-schema-specs/process-v2.md v2.0. Additive; preserves every
v0.4 field, the four-value process_classification lifecycle, the eight
endpoints, and the master pane verbatim.

Migration 0018:
- Adds six new TEXT NULL columns to processes: process_steps,
  process_triggers, process_outcomes, process_edge_cases,
  process_frequency, process_duration_estimate.
- Extends refs.relationship_kind CHECK to admit
  process_performed_by_persona, process_touches_field, and
  process_touches_entity.
- Reversible: downgrade drops the six columns and reverts the CHECK
  (with a guarded DELETE of any rows holding the new kinds — lossy
  recovery posture documented in the function docstring).

vocab.py:
- Adds the three new kinds to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS.
- Adds _kinds_for_pair clauses for (process, persona),
  (process, field), (process, entity). All three target types are
  live in ENTITY_TYPES (persona PI-003, field PI-004 first slice,
  entity v0.4) so every clause activates immediately.

Model, repo, schemas, router:
- Process model gains six Mapped[str | None] columns.
- create_process / update_process / patch_process accept the new
  optional kwargs; _PATCHABLE_FIELDS extended with the six new keys.
- ProcessCreateIn / ReplaceIn / PatchIn schemas accept the six new
  optional fields with docstrings noting PUT-clear semantics under
  full replace and PATCH-null/empty-string semantics.
- /processes router passes the new fields through on POST and PUT;
  PATCH unchanged (the dispatcher already strips the process_
  prefix from any provided key).

UI:
- Detail pane gains a "Phase 3 — Detailed Process Definition" group
  with six CollapsibleSections (Steps, Triggers, Outcomes, Edge
  Cases, Frequency, Duration). Each section's body collapses by
  default when its column is NULL/empty.
- Edit dialog includes editors for the six new fields via the new
  include_phase3 keyword on process_fields(). Create dialog
  unchanged per spec §3.6.4 (Phase 3 content is post-create work).

Tests (+22 new across three files, 0 regressions):
- tests/crmbuilder_v2/access/test_process.py — v0.4-record survival,
  per-field PATCH (parametrized × 6), PATCH-to-None clears,
  PATCH-to-empty-string preserves empty, PUT-omit clears, vocab
  admits new kinds, refs CHECK admits new kinds, process_touches_
  entity round-trip, process_performed_by_persona round-trip (guard-
  skipped when personas table absent), process_touches_field
  round-trip (guard-skipped when fields table absent),
  requirement_realized_by_process inbound surface check.
- tests/crmbuilder_v2/api/test_processes_api.py — Phase 3 fields
  round-trip on POST/GET, v0.4 record returns null Phase 3 fields,
  PATCH-null clears, PATCH-empty-string preserves, PUT-omit clears.
- tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_processes_panel.py — detail-pane
  field-order expectation updated to include the six new
  process_*_value object names between rationale and notes.

Baseline 1854 → 1876 passed (+22 new), 3 skipped pre-existing.

PI-005 resolves on close-out apply.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: Process v2 schema growth — six plain-TEXT Phase 3 content columns; structured representations deferred to v0.7+
decisions.context: PI-005 satisfier build per process-v2.md §3.2.2 / §3.9.1. v0.4 shipped the process entity's thin shape (identifier + name + domain FK + purpose + classification + classification_rationale + notes); v2 grows it with Phase 3 detailed-process content. The shape question is whether the new fields are plain TEXT, structured JSON, or first-class child records (`step` entity type).
decisions.decision: v0.8 ships six new TEXT NULL columns on the existing `processes` table — `process_steps`, `process_triggers`, `process_outcomes`, `process_edge_cases`, `process_frequency`, `process_duration_estimate`. All six are plain TEXT, nullable, default NULL, no length cap, no CHECK constraints. Existing v0.4 records acquire NULL on migration and remain valid. Structured representations (per-step actor / entity / outcome columns; frequency-as-cron; duration-as-ISO-8601-period; step-as-first-class-record entity type with its own table and CRUD) are explicitly deferred to v0.7+ pending CBM-redo real-use signal per spec §3.2.2 / §3.8.3.
decisions.rationale: Mirrors the v0.4 posture for `process_purpose` and `process_classification_rationale`: the methodology hasn't yet produced enough real-use signal to know which structured representations would help or constrain. Authoring structure prematurely risks shipping the wrong structure and forcing a painful migration. Plain TEXT is the additive, growth-ready choice — six columns rather than one to encode the methodological framing (the consultant authors steps, triggers, outcomes, edge cases, frequency, duration as separate concerns) without committing to a structured form. The cost is six columns rather than one consolidated body field; the value is uniform shape that pays off in detail-pane rendering (six independently-collapsible sections) and acceptance-criterion clarity. No required transitions among the new fields — a record may have any subset populated; the methodology does not impose an authoring order.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Single consolidated `process_body` free-text field (rejected; loses the methodological framing — Phase 3 is six things, not one). Structured JSON shape per field (e.g., per-step actor / entity / outcome in a JSON-array column for `process_steps`; rejected for v0.8 — premature without real-use signal). Step-as-first-class-record entity type with `STEP-NNN` identifiers and a `step_belongs_to_process` references-entity edge (rejected for v0.8 — larger scope; deferred to v0.7+ per §3.8.3 PI candidate). Length caps on text fields (rejected for v0.8+; pathological-input handling deferred to CBM-redo signal per §3.2.2). Frequency / duration as native datetime / interval types (rejected — frequency is often qualitative 'on-demand'-or-'quarterly' rather than periodic; duration distinguishes active human time from total elapsed time which a single datetime can't express).
decisions.consequences: Consultants author Phase 3 content as six independent plain-text fields. The detail pane renders them as six independently-collapsible sections per spec §3.6.3. Future v0.7+ planning items track the structured-form question per category (structured `process_steps` JSON vs step-as-first-class-record per spec §3.8.3) — neither is authored as a PI in v0.8 since the demand is hypothetical pending CBM-redo signal. Cross-engagement reporting and future server-side filters (e.g., `?frequency=quarterly`) cannot operate on the plain TEXT shape directly — also v0.7+ work.

decisions.title: Process v2 — three new outbound vocabulary kinds; `process_touches_entity` promoted from v0.4-anticipated to live registration
decisions.context: PI-005 satisfier build per process-v2.md §3.3.2 / §3.9.1. v0.4 shipped one outbound kind for the process entity (`process_hands_off_to_process`, source=target=process); §3.3.2 of the v0.4 spec anticipated three additional outbound kinds for the v0.5+ schema growth — to persona (PI-003), to field (PI-004 first slice), to entity (already in `ENTITY_TYPES` since v0.4). All three target types are live in `ENTITY_TYPES` by the time this build runs.
decisions.decision: Register three new outbound reference kinds in `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS`: `process_performed_by_persona` (process → persona; many-to-many), `process_touches_field` (process → field; many-to-many), `process_touches_entity` (process → entity; many-to-many). All three are admitted in the `refs.relationship_kind` CHECK via migration 0018. All three `_kinds_for_pair` clauses ship active because every target type is live in `ENTITY_TYPES`. The `(requirement, process)` inbound `requirement_realized_by_process` kind is registered on the requirement side per CLAUDE.md line 48's once-per-kind rule (already in `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS` since the PI-004 requirement build) and is not re-registered here.
decisions.rationale: All three relationships are many-to-many, so multi-value FK columns on the `processes` table would either need a side table (effectively the references table without the vocabulary-governance benefits) or denormalized comma-separated columns (not v2 convention). The references entity is the established v2 mechanism for many-to-many cross-entity-type edges per DEC-006. Existing references-section UI widgets render the new edges with no widget rewrite. Reverse queries (which processes touch this field?, which processes does this persona perform?) are trivial via the existing references-table reverse lookup. The `process_touches_entity` kind was anticipated in `process.md` §3.3.2 as v0.5+ work; v0.8 ships it as a live registration because its target type is already in `ENTITY_TYPES` since v0.4 and the methodology benefit (consultants may know entity-level touches before drilling down to specific fields) is non-trivial. Fine-grained read/write distinction (`process_reads_field` / `process_creates_field` / `process_updates_field` / `process_deletes_field`) deferred to v0.7+ per spec §3.8.2 pending CBM-redo signal.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Decompose `process_touches_field` into four kinds (read/create/update/delete) at v0.8 (rejected; too speculative without real-use signal — CBM-redo will surface whether the distinction matters; if so, v0.7+ decomposes). Skip `process_touches_entity` and only ship the field-level kind (rejected; consultants surface entity-level touches before field-level in real Phase 3 work, so the coarser kind is methodologically necessary). Direct FK columns on `processes` for persona/field/entity references (rejected; many-to-many doesn't fit single-FK shape; multi-value FK columns aren't v2 convention). Register kinds without activating clauses (rejected; all three target types are live so the dormancy convention has no purpose here).
decisions.consequences: Three new edges per process: `process_performed_by_persona` (who performs the process), `process_touches_field` (which fine-grained data the process touches), `process_touches_entity` (which entity types the process touches). Cardinality is many-to-many in both directions; zero edges permitted as the default state at Phase 1 capture. Soft-deleting a process does not cascade-delete its outbound references of any of the three new kinds (per spec §3.3.2 / §3.4.5). Soft-deleting the target (a persona, field, or entity) leaves the reference in place pointing to a now-soft-deleted target; the UI surfaces this with a warning per the existing posture for `process_belongs_to_domain`. Self-loops are not meaningful for any of the three new kinds and the UI's reference-create dialog filters by `(source_type, target_type)` so they are unreachable through normal authoring.

decisions.title: Process v2 — UI surface growth — Edit dialog includes Phase 3 editors, Create dialog excludes them; bundled-widget refactor declined for v0.8
decisions.context: PI-005 satisfier build per process-v2.md §3.6.3 / §3.6.4 / §3.6.5. The v2 UI grows the detail pane and the CRUD dialogs to host the six new Phase 3 content fields. The create-vs-edit asymmetry question and the optional bundled-widget refactor are both build-conversation decisions per spec §3.8.1.
decisions.decision: Asymmetric create-vs-edit dialog surfaces: the Edit dialog includes editors for all six new Phase 3 content fields; the Create dialog does NOT include them — per spec §3.6.4 Phase 3 content is post-create work. Implementation mechanism: a new `include_phase3: bool = False` keyword on `process_fields()` (defaults to False to preserve Create dialog behavior); `ProcessEditDialog` calls `process_fields(client, include_identifier=True, include_phase3=True)`; `ProcessCreateDialog` calls `process_fields(client, include_identifier=False)` (unchanged). Detail-pane Phase 3 sections per spec §3.6.3 ship as six inline `CollapsibleSection` widgets in `render_detail` keyed by a module-level `_PHASE3_SECTION_SPECS` list. The optional `ProcessExecutionContextSection` composite widget refactor (spec §3.6.3 / §3.8.1) is declined for v0.8 — the inline-rendering approach is sufficient and the bundled-widget refactor is a localized improvement reachable in any future iteration without touching the methodological surface.
decisions.rationale: Create-then-edit pattern matches the v0.4 / v0.5+ posture for every methodology entity with optional content fields: identifier-determinable inputs (name, domain, purpose, classification) at create time; everything else at edit time. Phase 3 content is methodologically post-create — a consultant authors it after the process record exists and is being elaborated, not as part of the create gesture. Asymmetric mechanism via `include_phase3` keyword on the shared `process_fields()` builder keeps the v0.4 shape intact and adds the Phase 3 fields as a single localized extension. Inline rendering rather than bundled-widget refactor avoids introducing a new widget class for a v0.8 build where the structure is well-bounded (six sections); the bundled-widget refactor pays off later when the structure changes (e.g., a seventh content field; collapse-state persistence; per-section markdown rendering) and can be done then without touching the methodological surface.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Symmetric Create and Edit dialogs both with Phase 3 editors (rejected per spec §3.6.4 — Phase 3 content is post-create work; mixed-shape create dialogs are a UX anti-pattern). Symmetric Create and Edit dialogs both without Phase 3 editors (rejected — would force operators to type into the API or use a downstream tool to populate Phase 3 fields; not in keeping with v2's desktop-first authoring model). Bundle the six sections into a `ProcessExecutionContextSection` composite widget at v0.8 (rejected — the inline-rendering approach is correct per the spec and the bundled-widget refactor adds maintenance surface without immediate value). Insert Phase 3 sections between identifier and name (rejected — methodologically Phase 3 is the elaborated tail end of the record per spec §3.6.3, not the lead).
decisions.consequences: Create dialog stays as the v0.4 shape (identifier omitted, six v0.4 fields, no Phase 3 editors). Edit dialog grows by six text editors at the end of the form (after the v0.4 notes section per the schema's natural appendage order). Detail pane grows by six collapsible sections between the rationale row and the notes section; each section's body collapses by default when its underlying column is NULL/empty (data-driven collapse state, not user-persisted per spec §3.8.2 — collapse-state persistence is a CBM-redo signal candidate for v0.7+). Object names follow the existing `process_<field>_value` / `process_<field>_section` convention so the panel test's widget-ordering expectation can be extended mechanically. The bundled-widget refactor remains reachable in any future build with a one-PR scope.
